expectedRestorationTime: Expected restoration time usually 3x-5x longer than backup duration (sometimes less, sometimes more depending on data type)
soItIsExpectedToTakeUpTo: So it is expected to take up to
thisErrorTypicallyOccurs: This error typically occurs when restoring to managed PostgreSQL services (like Yandex Cloud, AWS RDS or similar). Try enabling
extensionOwnerErrorHint: This error typically occurs when restoring to managed PostgreSQL services (like Yandex Cloud, AWS RDS or similar). Try enabling "Exclude extensions" in Advanced settings before restoring.
cancelRestoreWarning:
⚠️ Warning: Cancelling this restore will likely leave your database in a corrupted or incomplete state. You will need to recreate the database before attempting another restore.

Are you sure you want to cancel?
enterRestoreDbInfo: Enter info of the database we will restore backup to.
enterInfoOfDatabaseWeWillRestoreBackupTo: Enter info of database we will restore backup to.
emptyDbShouldBeCreated: The empty database for restore should be created before the restore.
theEmptyDatabaseForRestoreShouldBeCreate: The empty database for restore should be created before restore.
duringRestoreDataCleared: During the restore, all the current data will be cleared.
duringTheRestoreAllCurrentDataWillBeClea: During the restore, all current data will be cleared
makeSureDbNotUsed: Make sure the database is not used right now (most likely you do not want to restore the data to the same DB where the backup was made)
makeSureDatabaseIsNotUsedRightNow: Make sure database is not used right now (most likely you do not want to restore data to same DB where backup was made)
expectedRestoreTimeHint: Expected restoration time usually 3x-5x longer than the backup duration (sometimes less, sometimes more depending on data type)
expectedToTake: So it is expected to take up to {{time}} (usually significantly faster)
